textEn: A merchant has 1000kg of sugar, part of which he sells at 8% profit and the rest at 18% profit. He gains 14% on the whole. The quantity sold at 18% profit is:
textHi: एक व्यापारी के पास 1000 किग्रा चीनी है, जिसका कुछ भाग वह 8% लाभ पर और शेष भाग 18% लाभ पर बेचता है। उसे कुल मिलाकर 14% का लाभ होता है। 18% लाभ पर बेची गई मात्रा है:
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Ratio of quantities sold = (18 − 14) : (14 − 8)

Ratio = 4 : 6 = 2 : 3

Total units = 2 + 3 = 5 units → 1000 kg

1 unit = 200 kg

Quantity sold at 18% profit (3 units) = 3 × 200 = 600 kg

∴ The correct option is B.

Alternate Method

Given:

Total Sugar = 1000 kg

Profit on Part 1 = 8%

Profit on Part 2 = 18%

Overall Profit = 14%

Formula Used:

Ratio of Quantities = (Higher Profit % − Mean Profit %) : (Mean Profit % − Lower Profit %)

Calculations:

⇒ Quantity Ratio = (18 − 14) : (14 − 8)

⇒ Ratio = 4 : 6 = 2 : 3

⇒ Total weight parts = 2 + 3 = 5 parts

⇒ 5 units = 1000 kg

⇒ 1 unit = 1000 ÷ 5 = 200 kg

⇒ Quantity sold at 18% profit = 3 units

⇒ Quantity = 3 × 200 = 600 kg

∴ The correct option is B.

Additional Information

Rule of Alligation

It is used to find the ratio in which two components must be mixed to get a mixture of a desired value: (Quantity 1 / Quantity 2) = (Value 2 − Mean) / (Mean − Value 1).

Profit and Cost Price

Profit % is always calculated on the Cost Price (CP) unless stated otherwise. Profit = SP − CP.

Mean Value

The mean profit or price always lies between the two individual values (8 < 14 < 18).
explanationHi:
शॉर्टकट ट्रिक

बेची गई मात्राओं का अनुपात = (18 − 14) : (14 − 8)

अनुपात = 4 : 6 = 2 : 3

कुल इकाइयाँ = 2 + 3 = 5 इकाइयाँ → 1000 किग्रा

1 इकाई = 200 किग्रा

18% लाभ पर बेची गई मात्रा (3 इकाइयाँ) = 3 x 200 = 600 किग्रा

∴ सही विकल्प B है।

वैकल्पिक विधि

दिया गया है:

कुल चीनी = 1000 किग्रा

भाग 1 पर लाभ = 8%

भाग 2 पर लाभ = 18%

कुल लाभ = 14%

प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

मात्राओं का अनुपात = (उच्च लाभ % − माध्य लाभ %) : (माध्य लाभ % − निम्न लाभ %)

गणना:

⇒ मात्रा का अनुपात = (18 − 14) : (14 − 8)

⇒ अनुपात = 4 : 6 = 2 : 3

⇒ कुल वजन के भाग = 2 + 3 = 5 भाग

⇒ 5 इकाइयाँ = 1000 किग्रा

⇒ 1 इकाई = 1000 ÷ 5 = 200 किग्रा

⇒ 18% लाभ पर बेची गई मात्रा = 3 इकाइयाँ

⇒ मात्रा = 3 x 200 = 600 किग्रा

∴ सही विकल्प B है।

अतिरिक्त जानकारी

मिश्रण का नियम (Rule of Alligation)

इसका उपयोग उस अनुपात को ज्ञात करने के लिए किया जाता है जिसमें वांछित मान का मिश्रण प्राप्त करने के लिए दो घटकों को मिलाया जाना चाहिए: (मात्रा 1 / मात्रा 2) = (मान 2 − माध्य) / (माध्य − मान 1)।

लाभ और क्रय मूल्य

लाभ % की गणना हमेशा क्रय मूल्य (CP) पर की जाती है जब तक कि अन्यथा न कहा गया हो। लाभ = SP − CP।

माध्य मान

माध्य लाभ या मूल्य हमेशा दो व्यक्तिगत मानों के बीच होता है (8 < 14 < 18)।

textEn: A solid metallic cube of side 6 \(\sqrt[{3}]{4} \) cm, is melted and recast into a cuboid of length 12 cm and breadth 9 cm. What is the length (in cm) of the longest diagonal of the cuboid?
textHi: \(6 \sqrt[3]4 \) सेमी भुजा वाले धातु के एक ठोस घन को पिघलाकर 12 सेमी लम्बाई और 9 सेमी चौड़ाई वाला एक घनाभ बनाया जाता है। घनाभ के सबसे लंबे विकर्ण की लंबाई (सेमी में) क्या है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

When a solid is melted, Volume of Cube = Volume of Cuboid

⇒ (6 × 3√4)3 = 12 × 9 × h

⇒ 216 × 4 = 108 × h ⇒ h = 8 cm

Longest diagonal = √(l2 + b2 + h2) = √(122 + 92 + 82)

⇒ Longest diagonal = √(144 + 81 + 64) = √289 = 17 cm.

∴ The correct answer is 17 cm.

Alternate Method

Given:

Side of the cube (a) = 6 × 3√4 cm

Length of the cuboid (l) = 12 cm

Breadth of the cuboid (b) = 9 cm

Formula Used:

Volume of a cube = a3

Volume of a cuboid = l × b × h

Longest diagonal of a cuboid = √(l2 + b2 + h2)

Calculations:

⇒ Since the solid metallic cube is melted and recast into a cuboid, their volumes will be equal.

⇒ Volume of Cube = Volume of Cuboid

⇒ a3 = l × b × h

⇒ (6 × 3√4)3 = 12 × 9 × h

⇒ 63 × (3√4)3 = 108 × h

⇒ 216 × 4 = 108 × h

⇒ 864 = 108 × h

⇒ h = 864 ÷ 108

⇒ h = 8 cm

Now, substitute the values of l, b, and h to find the length of the longest diagonal:

⇒ Longest diagonal = √(122 + 92 + 82)

⇒ Longest diagonal = √(144 + 81 + 64)

⇒ Longest diagonal = √289

⇒ Longest diagonal = 17 cm

∴ The correct answer is 17 cm.

Additional Information

Melting and Recasting

When any 3D solid is melted to form a new solid, the total mass and volume remain perfectly conserved. (Volume of original solid = Volume of new solid)

Properties of a Cube

Volume = a3 | Total Surface Area = 6a2 | Body Diagonal = a√3

Properties of a Cuboid

Volume = l × b × h | Total Surface Area = 2(lb + bh + hl) | Longest Diagonal = √(l2 + b2 + h2)
explanationHi:
दिया है:

घन की भुजा = \(6 \sqrt[3]4 \) सेमी

घनाभ की लम्बाई = 12 सेमी

घनाभ की चौड़ाई = 9 सेमी

प्रयुक्त अवधारणा:

घन का आयतन = \(( side )^3\)

घनाभ का आयतन = लम्बाई × चौड़ाई × ऊँचाई

घनाभ के विकर्ण की लम्बाई = \(\sqrt{l^2 +b^2+h^2} \)

गणना:

⇒ माना विकर्ण की लम्बाई d सेमी है

⇒ जब आप एक ठोस आकृति को दूसरी ठोस आकृति में ढालते हैं, तो उनका आयतन समान रहता है, चाहे नया आकार कितना भी भिन्न क्यों न हो।

⇒ अतः घन का आयतन = घनाभ का आयतन

⇒ \((6\sqrt[3]4)^3\) = 12 × 9 × ऊँचाई

⇒ ऊँचाई = \(6 \times 6\times 6\times 4 \over 12\times 9\) = 8 सेमी

⇒ d = \(\sqrt{12^2 + 9^2 + 8^2}\)

⇒ d = \(\sqrt {144 + 81 + 64 }\) = \(\sqrt {289}\)

⇒ d = 17 सेमी

अतः, विकर्ण की लम्बाई 17 सेमी है।

textEn: What is the average of first 20 Natural even numbers?
textHi: प्रथम 20 प्राकृतिक सम संख्याओं का औसत क्या है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

The shortcut formula for the average of the first n even natural numbers is (n + 1).

Given n = 20.

Average = 20 + 1 = 21.

∴ The correct option is C.

Alternate Method

Given: The first 20 even natural numbers are 2, 4, 6, ..., up to the 20th term.

Formula Used: Average of an Arithmetic Progression (AP) = (First term + Last term) ÷ 2

⇒ First term (a) = 2

⇒ Last term (l) = 2 × 20 = 40

⇒ Average = (2 + 40) ÷ 2

⇒ Average = 42 ÷ 2 = 21

∴ The correct option is C.

Additional Information

Average of first n natural numbers

The average of the first n natural numbers (1, 2, 3, ...) is given by (n + 1) ÷ 2.

Average of first n odd natural numbers

The average of the first n odd natural numbers (1, 3, 5, ...) is exactly equal to n.

Sum of first n even natural numbers

The sum of the first n even natural numbers is calculated using the formula n × (n + 1).
explanationHi:
शॉर्टकट ट्रिक

प्रथम n सम प्राकृत संख्याओं के औसत का शॉर्टकट सूत्र (n + 1) है।

दिया गया है n = 20.

औसत = 20 + 1 = 21.

∴ सही विकल्प C है।

वैकल्पिक विधि

दिया गया है: प्रथम 20 सम प्राकृत संख्याएँ 2, 4, 6, ..., 20वें पद तक हैं।

प्रयुक्त सूत्र: समांतर श्रेढ़ी (AP) का औसत = (प्रथम पद + अंतिम पद) ÷ 2

⇒ प्रथम पद (a) = 2

⇒ अंतिम पद (l) = 2 x 20 = 40

⇒ औसत = (2 + 40) ÷ 2

⇒ औसत = 42 ÷ 2 = 21

∴ सही विकल्प C है।

अतिरिक्त जानकारी

प्रथम n प्राकृत संख्याओं का औसत

प्रथम n प्राकृत संख्याओं (1, 2, 3, ...) का औसत (n + 1) ÷ 2 द्वारा दिया जाता है।

प्रथम n विषम प्राकृत संख्याओं का औसत

प्रथम n विषम प्राकृत संख्याओं (1, 3, 5, ...) का औसत ठीक n के बराबर होता है।

प्रथम n सम प्राकृत संख्याओं का योग

प्रथम n सम प्राकृत संख्याओं के योग की गणना n x (n + 1) सूत्र का उपयोग करके की जाती है।

textEn: The length and the breadth of a rectangular cardboard are 40 cm and 35 cm, respectively. If the biggest possible square is cut out from this cardboard, what is the area of the remaining part of the cardboard?
textHi: एक आयताकार कार्डबोर्ड की लम्बाई और चौड़ाई क्रमशः 40 सेमी और 35 सेमी है। यदि इस कार्डबोर्ड से सबसे बड़ा संभावित वर्ग काट दिया जाए, तो कार्डबोर्ड के शेष भाग का क्षेत्रफल क्या है?
optionsEn: 275 cm2
425 cm2
325 cm2
optionsHi: <font style="vertical-align: inherit;"><font style="vertical-align: inherit;">175 सेमी</font></font><sup><font style="vertical-align: inherit;"><font style="vertical-align: inherit;">2</font></font></sup>
275 सेमी2
425 सेमी2
325 सेमी2
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

To cut out the biggest possible square from a rectangle, its side must equal the smaller dimension (breadth).

Side of the square = 35 cm.

The remaining portion is a rectangle with length (40 − 35) = 5 cm and breadth 35 cm.

Remaining Area = 5 × 35 = 175 cm2.

∴ The correct answer is 175 cm2.

Alternate Method

Given:

Length of cardboard (L) = 40 cm

Breadth of cardboard (B) = 35 cm

Formula Used:

Area of rectangle = Length × Breadth

Area of square = Side × Side

Calculations:

⇒ Area of the original cardboard = 40 × 35 = 1400 cm2

⇒ For the biggest possible square, Side (a) = Breadth = 35 cm

⇒ Area of the cut-out square = 35 × 35 = 1225 cm2

⇒ Area of the remaining part = Area of original cardboard − Area of cut-out square

⇒ Area of the remaining part = 1400 − 1225 = 175 cm2

∴ The correct answer is 175 cm2.

Additional Information

Maximum Square from a Rectangle

To cut the largest possible square from a rectangle, the side length of the square must be perfectly equal to the shorter side (breadth) of the given rectangle.

Area of a Rectangle

The area of a rectangle is defined as the product of its length and breadth. Formula: Area = L × B.

Perimeter of a Rectangle

The total distance around the outside boundary of a rectangle. Formula: Perimeter = 2 × (L + B).
explanationHi:
दिया है:

एक आयताकार कार्डबोर्ड की लम्बाई और चौड़ाई क्रमशः 40 सेमी और 35 सेमी है।

प्रयुक्त अवधारणा:

1. एक वर्ग का क्षेत्रफल = (भुजा)2 वर्ग इकाई

2. आयत का क्षेत्रफल = लम्बाई × चौड़ाई

गणना:

कार्डबोर्ड का क्षेत्रफल = 40 × 35 = 1400 सेमी2

चूँकि, चौड़ाई 35 सेमी है, वर्ग की प्रत्येक भुजा की अधिकतम माप = 35 सेमी

इसलिए, वर्ग का क्षेत्रफल = 352 = 1225 सेमी2

अब, कार्डबोर्ड के शेष भाग का क्षेत्रफल = 1400 - 1225 = 175 सेमी2

∴ इस कार्डबोर्ड से सबसे बड़ा संभावित वर्ग काटा जाता है, तो कार्डबोर्ड के शेष भाग का क्षेत्रफल 175 सेमी2 होगा।

textEn: An arc on a circle that is 15 cm long subtends a 24° angle at the centre. What is the circumference of the circle?
textHi: 15 सेमी लंबा एक चाप, वृत्त के केंद्र पर 24° का कोण अंतरित करता है। वृत्त की परिधि कितनी है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

The total circumference of a circle corresponds to a central angle of 360°.

An angle of 24° corresponds to an arc length of 15 cm.

Therefore, the full 360° corresponds to: (360° ÷ 24°) × 15 cm.

Circumference = 15 × 15 = 225 cm.

∴ The correct answer is 225 cm.

Alternate Method

Given:

Length of arc (l) = 15 cm

Angle subtended at the centre (θ) = 24°

Formula Used:

Length of an arc = (θ ÷ 360°) × Circumference

Calculations:

⇒ 15 = (24° ÷ 360°) × Circumference

⇒ 15 = (1 ÷ 15) × Circumference

⇒ Circumference = 15 × 15

⇒ Circumference = 225 cm

∴ The correct answer is 225 cm.

Additional Information

Area of a Sector

The area of a sector of a circle with radius r and central angle θ is given by Area = (θ ÷ 360°) × πr2.

Circumference of a Circle

The total boundary length of a circle is calculated as C = 2πr or C = πd, which corresponds to the full 360° arc.

Radian Measure

When measuring angles in radians, the formula for arc length is simplified to l = r × θ. A complete circle is 2π radians.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

चाप की लंबाई = 15 सेमी

चाप द्वारा केंद्र पर बनाया गया कोण = 24°

प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

चाप की लंबाई = \(\frac{θ }{360°}\) × 2πr

जहाँ, 2πr = परिधि और θ = चाप द्वारा केंद्र में अंतरित कोण

गणना:

प्रश्नानुसार,

⇒ \(\frac{\theta }{360°}\) × 2πr = Length of the arc

⇒ \(\frac{24°}{360°}\) × 2πr = 15

⇒ 2πr = 15 × 15

⇒ 2πr = 225 cm

∴ परिधि की लंबाई 225 सेमी है।

textEn: If the mean of five observations x, x + 2, x + 4, x + 6 and x + 8 is 11, then the mean of the last three observations is:
textHi: यदि पाँच प्रेक्षणों x, x + 2, x + 4, x + 6 तथा x + 8 का माध्य 11 है, तो अंतिम तीन प्रेक्षणों का माध्य क्या है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

The given observations are in an Arithmetic Progression (A.P.) with a common difference of 2.

For an odd number of terms in an A.P., the mean is exactly the middle term.

The middle term of the 5 observations is (x + 4), so x + 4 = 11 ⇒ x = 7.

The last three observations are (x + 4), (x + 6) and (x + 8).

The mean of these 3 terms is their middle term, which is (x + 6) = 7 + 6 = 13.

∴ The correct answer is 13.

Alternate Method

Given:

Observations: x, x + 2, x + 4, x + 6, x + 8

Mean of these 5 observations = 11

Formula Used:

Mean = Sum of all observations ÷ Total number of observations

Calculations:

⇒ Mean = (x + x + 2 + x + 4 + x + 6 + x + 8) ÷ 5

⇒ 11 = (5x + 20) ÷ 5

⇒ 11 = x + 4

⇒ x = 11 − 4

⇒ x = 7

⇒ The last three observations are:

⇒ x + 4 = 7 + 4 = 11

⇒ x + 6 = 7 + 6 = 13

⇒ x + 8 = 7 + 8 = 15

⇒ Mean of last three observations = (11 + 13 + 15) ÷ 3

⇒ Mean = 39 ÷ 3 = 13

∴ The correct answer is 13.

Additional Information

Mean of an Arithmetic Progression (A.P.)

When a set of numbers is in an A.P., their mean is always equal to the exact middle term (if the number of terms is odd) or the average of the two middle terms (if even). It is also equal to (First Term + Last Term) ÷ 2.

Change in Average with Fixed Addition

If a constant value 'k' is added to or subtracted from every observation in a dataset, the new mean of the observations will also identically increase or decrease by exactly 'k'.

Sum of Consecutive Terms

The sum of 'n' terms in an A.P. can be quickly calculated as n × Average. For instance, the sum of 5 terms with an average of 11 is directly 5 × 11 = 55.
explanationHi:
दिया गया:

यदि पाँच प्रेक्षणों x, x + 2, x + 4, x + 6 और x + 8 का माध्य 11 है

प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

माध्य = (प्रेक्षणों का योग) / (प्रेक्षणों की कुल संख्या)

गणना:

सूत्र के अनुसार,

माध्य = (प्रेक्षणों का योग) / (प्रेक्षणों की कुल संख्या)

⇒ 11 = (x \(+\) x \(+\) 2 \(+\) x \(+\) 4 \(+\) x \(+\) 6 \(+\) x \(+\) 8) \(/\) 5

⇒ 55 = 5x \(+\) 20

⇒ 5x = 55 - 20

⇒ 5x = 35

⇒ x = 7

⇒ पहली संख्या = x = 7

⇒ दूसरी संख्या = x \(+\) 2 = 7 \(+\) 2 = 9

⇒ तीसरी संख्या = x \(+\) 4 = 7 \(+\) 4 = 11

⇒ चौथी संख्या = x \(+\) 6 = 7 \(+\) 6 = 13

⇒ पांचवीं संख्या = x \(+\) 8 = 7 \(+\) 8 = 15

अंतिम तीन संख्याओं का माध्य है

⇒ माध्य = (प्रेक्षणों का योग) / (प्रेक्षणों की कुल संख्या) = (11 + 13 + 15) \(/\) 3 = 39 \(/\) 3 = 13

∴ अंतिम तीन संख्याओं का माध्य 13 है।

textEn: A train leaves Hyderabad at 4:00 A.M. and reaches Vijayawada at 8:00 A.M. the same day. Another train leaves Vijayawada at 6:00 A.M. and reaches Hyderabad at 9:30 A.M the same day. At what time do the two trains cross each other?
textHi: एक ट्रेन हैदराबाद से सुबह 4:00 बजे निकलती है और उसी दिन सुबह 8:00 बजे विजयवाड़ा पहुँचती है। दूसरी ट्रेन विजयवाड़ा से सुबह 6:00 बजे निकलती है और उसी दिन सुबह 9:30 बजे हैदराबाद पहुँचती है। दोनों ट्रेनें किस समय एक-दूसरे को पार करती हैं?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Let total distance = LCM of 4 and 3.5 = 28 units. Speeds are 28 ÷ 4 = 7 units/hr and 28 ÷ 3.5 = 8 units/hr.

Train 1 travels from 4:00 A.M. to 6:00 A.M., covering 7 × 2 = 14 units.

Remaining distance = 28 − 14 = 14 units, and Relative speed = 7 + 8 = 15 units/hr.

Time to meet = 14 ÷ 15 hours = 56 minutes after 6:00 A.M., which is 6:56 A.M.

∴ The correct answer is 6:56 A.M.

Alternate Method

Given:

Train 1 timings: 4:00 A.M. to 8:00 A.M. (Time taken = 4 hours)

Train 2 timings: 6:00 A.M. to 9:30 A.M. (Time taken = 3.5 hours = 7/2 hours)

Formula Used:

Speed = Distance ÷ Time

Time to meet = Distance ÷ Relative Speed

Calculations:

⇒ Let the total distance between Hyderabad and Vijayawada be D = 28x km.

⇒ Speed of Train 1 = 28x ÷ 4 = 7x km/h

⇒ Speed of Train 2 = 28x ÷ (7/2) = 8x km/h

⇒ From 4:00 A.M. to 6:00 A.M. (2 hours), Train 1 covers distance = 2 × 7x = 14x km

⇒ Remaining distance at 6:00 A.M. = 28x − 14x = 14x km

⇒ As trains are moving in opposite directions, Relative Speed = 7x + 8x = 15x km/h

⇒ Time taken to cross each other = Remaining distance ÷ Relative Speed

⇒ Time taken = 14x ÷ 15x = 14/15 hours

⇒ Converting into minutes = (14 ÷ 15) × 60 = 56 minutes

⇒ They cross each other 56 minutes after 6:00 A.M. = 6:56 A.M.

∴ The correct answer is 6:56 A.M.

Additional Information

Relative Speed

When two bodies move towards each other (in opposite directions), their relative speed is the sum of their individual speeds (S1 + S2). When moving in the same direction, it is the difference (S1 − S2).

Time of Meeting

To calculate the exact time of meeting, always ensure both moving bodies start at the same reference time. The distance covered by the early starter must be subtracted from the total distance first.

Average Speed

When traveling the same distance at two different speeds S1 and S2, the Average Speed = (2 × S1 × S2) ÷ (S1 + S2).
explanationHi:
दिया गया:

एक ट्रेन हैदराबाद से सुबह 4:00 बजे निकलती है और उसी दिन सुबह 8:00 बजे विजयवाड़ा पहुंचती है। दूसरी ट्रेन विजयवाड़ा से सुबह 6:00 बजे निकलती है और उसी दिन सुबह 9:30 बजे हैदराबाद पहुंचती है।

गणना:

पहली ट्रेन द्वारा लिया गया समय = 4 घंटे (सुबह 4 बजे से सुबह 8 बजे तक)

दूसरी ट्रेन द्वारा लिया गया समय = 3.5 घंटे (सुबह 6 बजे से 9:30 बजे तक)

अब,

समय ⇒ 4 : 3.5

गति ⇒ 3.5 : 4

कुल दूरी = 4 × 3.5 = 14 इकाई

पहली ट्रेन द्वारा 2 घंटे में तय की गई दूरी (सुबह 4 बजे से 6 बजे तक) = 3.5 × 2 = 7 इकाई

शेष दूरी = 14 - 7 = 7 इकाई

अतः, दोनों ट्रेनों से मिलने का समय = D/सापेक्ष गति = 7/(4 + 3.5) = 7/7.5 घंटे = 70/75 * 60 मिनट = 56 मिनट

अतः, अपेक्षित उत्तर = 6:00 + 56 मिनट = 6:56 पूर्वाह्न

इसलिए सही उत्तर विकल्प (4) सुबह 6:56 है।

textEn: The ratio of income of two persons is 10 ∶ 6 and that of their expenditures is 18 ∶ 10. If they save ₹ 5,200 and ₹ 3,600 respectively, their incomes are :
textHi: दो व्यक्तियों की आय का अनुपात 10 ∶ 6 है और उनके व्यय का अनुपात 18 ∶ 10 है। यदि वे क्रमशः ₹ 5,200 और ₹ 3,600 बचाते हैं, तो उनकी आय क्या हैं ?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Using the cross-multiplication method:

Income ratio = 10 ∶ 6, Expenditure ratio = 18 ∶ 10, Savings = ₹ 5200 and ₹ 3600.

Cross-product difference of ratios = |10 × 10 − 18 × 6| = |100 − 108| = 8 units.

Cross-product difference of expenditures and savings = |18 × 3600 − 10 × 5200| = |64800 − 52000| = 12800.

8 units ≡ 12800 ⇒ 1 unit ≡ 1600.

∴ The correct answer is ₹ 16,000, ₹ 9,600.

Alternate Method

Given:

⇒ Let the incomes of the two persons be 10x and 6x respectively.

⇒ Let their expenditures be 18y and 10y respectively.

⇒ Income − Expenditure = Savings

⇒ Person 1 savings: 10x − 18y = 5200 ... (Equation 1)

⇒ Person 2 savings: 6x − 10y = 3600 ... (Equation 2)

Calculations:

⇒ Multiply Equation 1 by 5: 50x − 90y = 26000

⇒ Multiply Equation 2 by 9: 54x − 90y = 32400

⇒ Subtract the two equations: (54x − 50x) − (90y − 90y) = 32400 − 26000

⇒ 4x = 6400

⇒ x = 1600

⇒ Income of Person 1 = 10x = 10 × 1600 = ₹ 16,000

⇒ Income of Person 2 = 6x = 6 × 1600 = ₹ 9,600

∴ The correct answer is ₹ 16,000, ₹ 9,600.

Additional Information

Income, Expenditure & Savings Relation

The fundamental relation in such problems is always: Income = Expenditure + Savings. This basic equation allows you to construct linear equations from given ratios.

Cross-Multiplication Method

When both the initial quantities and their respective absolute changes (like numerical savings) are known, the cross-product differences between initial ratios and changes yield directly proportional equivalents, bypassing extensive algebra.
explanationHi:
दिया गया:

दो व्यक्तियों की आय का अनुपात = 10 : 6

उनके व्यय का अनुपात = 18 : 10 = 9 : 5

उनकी बचत ₹ 5,200 और ₹ 3,600

प्रयुक्त अवधारणा:

व्यय = आय - बचत

गणना:

मान लीजिए कि दोनों व्यक्तियों की आय क्रमशः 10x और 6x है,

प्रश्न के अनुसार,

\(\frac{10x - 5200}{6x - 3600}\) = \(\frac{18}{10}\) = \(\frac{9}{5}\)

⇒ 5(10x - 5200) = 9(6x - 3600)

⇒ 50x - 26000 = 54x - 32400

⇒ 54x - 50x = 32400 - 26000

⇒ 4x = 6400

⇒ x = 1600

इस प्रकार,

उनकी आय = 10x = ₹ 16000 और 6x = ₹ 9600

∴ सही उत्तर विकल्प (1) है।

textEn: What is the % profit on the cost price of an article if there is a profit of 20% on the selling price?
textHi: यदि किसी वस्तु के विक्रय मूल्य पर 20% का लाभ होता है, तो वस्तु के क्रय मूल्य पर% लाभ क्या है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Profit on Selling Price = 20% = 1/5

This means SP = 5 units, Profit = 1 unit.

Cost Price (CP) = SP − Profit = 5 − 1 = 4 units.

Actual Profit % = (Profit ÷ CP) × 100 = (1 ÷ 4) × 100 = 25%.

∴ The correct answer is 25%.

Alternate Method

Given:

Profit calculated on Selling Price (SP) = 20%

Formula Used:

CP = SP − Profit

Actual Profit % = (Profit ÷ CP) × 100

Calculations:

⇒ Let Selling Price (SP) = 100 units

⇒ Profit = 20% of 100 = 20 units

⇒ Cost Price (CP) = 100 − 20 = 80 units

⇒ Actual Profit % = (Profit ÷ CP) × 100

⇒ Actual Profit % = (20 ÷ 80) × 100

⇒ Actual Profit % = 1/4 × 100 = 25%

∴ The correct answer is 25%.

Additional Information

Loss Calculated on Selling Price

If a loss is given as x% on SP, then CP = SP + Loss. The actual loss percentage is (Loss ÷ CP) × 100.

Marked Price & Discount

Discount is always calculated on the Marked Price (MP) unless stated otherwise. Selling Price = MP − (Discount % of MP).

Dishonest Dealer

When a dealer sells at CP but uses false weight, Profit % = (Error ÷ (True Value − Error)) × 100.
explanationHi:
दिया गया :

लाभ = 20%

प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

क्रय मूल्य = विक्रय मूल्य - लाभ

क्रय मूल्य पर लाभ प्रतिशत = (लाभ / लागत मूल्य) × 100

गणना:

माना किसी वस्तु का विक्रय मूल्य 'x' है।

लाभ = विक्रय मूल्य का 20%

⇒ लाभ = 0.2x

अब,

क्रय मूल्य = विक्रय मूल्य - लाभ

⇒ क्रय मूल्य = x - 0.2x

⇒ क्रय मूल्य = 0.8x

क्रय मूल्य पर लाभ प्रतिशत = (लाभ / क्रय मूल्य) × 100

⇒ लाभ% = (0.2x / 0.8x) × 100

⇒ लाभ% = 25%

अत: सही उत्तर 25% है।

Alternate Method

माना किसी वस्तु का विक्रय मूल्य 100 है

लाभ = विक्रय मूल्य का 20%

⇒ लाभ = 20

अब,

क्रय मूल्य = विक्रय मूल्य - लाभ

⇒ क्रय मूल्य = 100 - 20

⇒ क्रय मूल्य = 80

क्रय मूल्य पर लाभ प्रतिशत = (लाभ / क्रय मूल्य) × 100

⇒ लाभ% = 20/80 × 100 = 25%

अत: सही उत्तर 25% है।

textEn: If the 11-digit number 79x6402087y is divisible by 72, then for smallest value of x, what is the value of (x + 2y)?
textHi: यदि 11 अंकों की संख्या 79x6402087y, 72 से विभाज्य है, तो x के न्यूनतम मान के लिए (x + 2y) का मान क्या होगा?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

For a number to be divisible by 72, it must be perfectly divisible by both 8 and 9.

Rule of 8: The last three digits (87y) must be divisible by 8 ⇒ 872 is divisible by 8, so y = 2.

Rule of 9: The sum of digits (45 + x) must be divisible by 9 ⇒ The smallest possible value for x is 0.

Required value = (x + 2y) = 0 + 2 × 2 = 4.

∴ The correct answer is 4.

Alternate Method

Given:

The 11-digit number 79x6402087y is divisible by 72.

Method Used:

If a number is divisible by 72, it must be divisible by its co-prime factors 8 and 9.

Divisibility rule of 8: The number formed by the last three digits must be divisible by 8.

Divisibility rule of 9: The sum of all the digits must be divisible by 9.

Calculations:

⇒ Consider the last 3 digits for divisibility by 8: 87y

⇒ 87y = 800 + 70 + y

⇒ 800 is perfectly divisible by 8, so (70 + y) must also be divisible by 8.

⇒ The closest multiple of 8 to 70 is 72, which gives y = 2.

⇒ So, y = 2. The number now becomes 79x64020872.

⇒ Now, apply the divisibility rule of 9 to the entire number.

⇒ Sum of digits = 7 + 9 + x + 6 + 4 + 0 + 2 + 0 + 8 + 7 + 2

⇒ Sum of digits = 45 + x

⇒ For (45 + x) to be divisible by 9, x can either be 0 or 9 (since 45 is a multiple of 9).

⇒ Since we need the smallest value of x, we take x = 0.

⇒ We need to find the final value of (x + 2y).

⇒ (x + 2y) = 0 + 2 × 2

⇒ (x + 2y) = 4

∴ The correct answer is 4.

Additional Information

Divisibility Rule of 8

A number is divisible by 8 if the number formed by its last three digits is perfectly divisible by 8.

Divisibility Rule of 9

A number is divisible by 9 if the sum of all its digits is a multiple of 9.

Divisibility Rule of 11

A number is divisible by 11 if the difference between the sum of digits at odd places and the sum of digits at even places is either 0 or a multiple of 11.

Co-prime Divisibility Theorem

If a number is divisible by two co-prime numbers a and b, it is always divisible by their product (a × b). For example, 72 = 8 × 9.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

79x6402087y, 72 से विभाज्य है,

प्रयुक्त अवधारणा:

8 के विभाज्यता का नियम = यदि किसी संख्या के अंतिम तीन अंक 8 से विभाज्य हैं, तो वह संख्या 8 से पूर्णतः विभाजित होगी

9 के विभाज्यता का नियम = यदि किसी संख्या के अंकों का योग 9 से विभाज्य हो, तो वह संख्या स्वयं 9 से विभाजित होगी

गणना:

72 = 8 × 9

इसलिए, संख्या 8 और 9 दोनों से विभाजित होंगे

यहाँ, 87y, 8 द्वारा विभजित होगा

y का संभावित मान 2 है

अब,

7 + 9 + x + 6 + 4 + 0 + 2 + 0 + 8 + 7 + 2 = (45 + x), 9 से विभजित होंगे

अब, 45 स्वयं 9 से विभजित होगी

इसलिए, x का न्यूनतम मान = 0

अब,

(x + 2y) = (0 + 2 × 2)

⇒ 4

∴ अभीष्ट उत्तर 4 है।

textEn: If Sec( 2a - 32°) = Cosec(46° - 21a) , then find the value of a?
textHi: यदि Sec( 2a - 32°) = Cosec(46° - 21a) है, तब a का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

If Sec(A) = Cosec(B), then A + B = 90°.

⇒ 2a − 32° + 46° − 21a = 90°

⇒ −19a + 14° = 90°

⇒ −19a = 76°

⇒ a = −4°

∴ The correct answer is −4°.

Alternate Method

Given:

Sec(2a − 32°) = Cosec(46° − 21a)

Formula Used:

Cosec(θ) = Sec(90° − θ)

Calculations:

⇒ Sec(2a − 32°) = Sec(90° − (46° − 21a))

⇒ 2a − 32° = 90° − 46° + 21a

⇒ 2a − 32° = 44° + 21a

⇒ 2a − 21a = 44° + 32°

⇒ −19a = 76°

⇒ a = −4°

∴ The correct answer is −4°.

Additional Information

Complementary Angles

Two angles are complementary if their sum is exactly 90°. Trigonometric co-functions of complementary angles are equal: Sin(A) = Cos(B), Tan(A) = Cot(B), and Sec(A) = Cosec(B) when A + B = 90°.

Pythagorean Identities

The fundamental identities are Sin2θ + Cos2θ = 1, 1 + Tan2θ = Sec2θ, and 1 + Cot2θ = Cosec2θ.

Signs in Quadrants

In the 1st quadrant (0° to 90°), all trigonometric ratios are positive. This ASTC rule helps in determining the sign of ratios for larger angles.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

Sec(2a - 32°) = Cosec(46° - 21a)

प्रयुक्त अवधारणा:

Sec θ = Cosec (90 - θ)

गणना:

Cosec {90° - ( 2a - 32°)} = Cosec (46° - 21a)

⇒ 90° - 2a + 32° = 46° - 21a

⇒ 122°- 2a = 46° - 21a

⇒ 21a - 2a = 46° - 122°

⇒ 19a = - 76°

⇒ a = - 76° / 19

⇒ a = - 4°

a का मान - 4° है।

textEn: A man invested 1/3 of his retirement gratuity at 6% simple interest; 1/4 of his gratuity at 7% and the rest at 8% simple interest. If his annual return on these investments is Rs. 7012.5, find the total amount of gratuity invested by the man.
textHi: एक व्यक्ति ने अपनी सेवानिवृत्ति आनुतोषिक का 1/3 हिस्सा 6% साधारण ब्याज पर, अपनी आनुतोषिक का 1/4 हिस्सा 7% साधारण ब्याज पर और शेष 8% साधारण ब्याज पर निवेश किया। यदि इन निवेशों पर उसका वार्षिक रिटर्न 7012.5 रुपये है, व्यक्ति द्वारा निवेश की गई आनुतोषिक की कुल राशि ज्ञात कीजिए।
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Let the total gratuity be 1200 units (LCM of 3 and 4 multiplied by 100 for easy percentage calculation).

Investment parts: 400 units (1/3rd), 300 units (1/4th), and remaining 500 units.

Total Interest = (400 × 6%) + (300 × 7%) + (500 × 8%) = 24 + 21 + 40 = 85 units.

Given 85 units = ₹7012.5 ⇒ 1 unit = 7012.5 ÷ 85 = ₹82.5

Total gratuity = 1200 units = 1200 × 82.5 = ₹99,000.

∴ The correct answer is ₹99,000.

Alternate Method

Given:

1/3 of gratuity invested at 6%

1/4 of gratuity invested at 7%

Rest of the gratuity invested at 8%

Total Annual Return (Simple Interest) = ₹7012.5

Formula Used:

Simple Interest (I) = (P × R × T) ÷ 100

Calculations:

⇒ Let the total gratuity be 1200x (for ease of calculation).

⇒ Fraction of principal at 8% = 1 − (1/3 + 1/4) = 1 − 7/12 = 5/12

⇒ Amount invested at 6% = (1/3) × 1200x = 400x

⇒ Amount invested at 7% = (1/4) × 1200x = 300x

⇒ Amount invested at 8% = (5/12) × 1200x = 500x

⇒ Total Simple Interest = [(400x × 6 × 1) ÷ 100] + [(300x × 7 × 1) ÷ 100] + [(500x × 8 × 1) ÷ 100]

⇒ 7012.5 = 24x + 21x + 40x

⇒ 7012.5 = 85x

⇒ x = 7012.5 ÷ 85

⇒ x = 82.5

⇒ Total Gratuity = 1200x = 1200 × 82.5 = 99000

∴ The correct answer is ₹99,000.

Additional Information

Simple Interest (SI)

It is calculated only on the initial principal for all years. The formula is SI = PRT/100, where P is Principal, R is Rate of Interest, and T is Time.

Effective Rate of Interest

When parts of principal P are invested at different rates R1, R2... the effective overall rate is the weighted average of the individual rates.

Compound Interest (CI)

Interest is calculated on both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods. The formula for the amount is A = P(1 + R/100)T.
explanationHi:
प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

साधारण ब्याज = (मूलधन × दर × समय)/100

गणना:

मान लें कि व्यक्ति द्वारा प्राप्त कुल सेवानिवृत्ति ग्रेच्युटी x है।

उसने 6% साधारण ब्याज पर निवेश की गई राशि = 1/3 × x

उसने 7% साधारण ब्याज पर निवेश की गई राशि = 1/4 × x

उसने 8% साधारण ब्याज पर निवेश की गई राशि = x - (1/3 × x) - (1/4 × x) = 5/12 × x

प्रश्न के अनुसार:

6% निवेश से ब्याज = (1/3 × x) × 6 × 1/100 = 2x/100

7% निवेश से ब्याज = (1/4 × x) × 7 × 1/100 = 1.75x/100

8% निवेश पर ब्याज = (5/12 × x) × 8 × 1/100 = 3.33x/100

अर्जित कुल ब्याज रु. 7012.5 के रूप में दिया गया है, इसलिए हम एक समीकरण स्थापित कर सकते हैं:

(2x + 1.75x + 3.33x)/100 = 7012.5

⇒ 7.08x/100 = 7012.5

⇒ x = 99000

∴ व्यक्ति द्वारा निवेश की गई उपदान की कुल राशि रु. 99000 है।

textEn: Sapna invests an amount of Rs.80000 to start a business. After 6 months Khyati joined his business with an amount of Rs.30000. If they got a profit of Rs.25500 after one year, then find the share of Khyati in the profit. (rounded off to the nearest rupee)
textHi: सपना ने बिजनेस शुरू करने के लिए 80000 रुपये की रकम निवेश की। 6 महीने के बाद ख्याति 30000 रुपये की राशि के साथ उसके व्यवसाय में शामिल हो गई। यदि उन्हें एक वर्ष के बाद 25500 रुपये का लाभ हुआ, तो लाभ में ख्याति का हिस्सा ज्ञात कीजिए। (निकटतम रुपये में पूर्णांकित)
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Profit ratio = (Capital1 × Time1) : (Capital2 × Time2)

Sapna : Khyati = (80000 × 12) : (30000 × 6) = 16 : 3

Total profit parts = 16 + 3 = 19 parts = 25500

Khyati's share = 3 parts = (25500 ÷ 19) × 3 ≈ 4026.31

∴ The correct answer is ₹4026.

Alternate Method

Given:

Sapna's Investment = ₹80,000

Time period of Sapna = 12 months (since she started the business)

Khyati's Investment = ₹30,000

Time period of Khyati = 12 − 6 = 6 months (since she joined after 6 months)

Total profit = ₹25,500

Formula Used:

Profit Ratio = (Investment1 × Time1) : (Investment2 × Time2)

Calculations:

⇒ Profit Ratio = (80000 × 12) : (30000 × 6)

⇒ Profit Ratio = 960000 : 180000

⇒ Profit Ratio = 96 : 18 = 16 : 3

⇒ Total ratio parts = 16 + 3 = 19

⇒ Khyati's share = (3 ÷ 19) × 25500

⇒ Khyati's share = 76500 ÷ 19 = 4026.315...

⇒ Khyati's share ≈ ₹4026 (rounded off to the nearest rupee)

∴ The correct answer is ₹4026.

Additional Information

Joining Mid-way in Partnership

When a partner joins after a few months, their investment time is calculated from their joining date until the end of the financial year (e.g., 12 − 6 = 6 months).

Compound Ratio in Profits

The profit ratio is the compound ratio of the investment amounts and the respective time periods. Profit = Capital × Time.

Sleeping vs Working Partner

A working partner actively manages the business and usually receives a salary or a fixed percentage of the profit before the remaining profit is distributed. A sleeping partner only invests capital.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

सपना ने 12 महीने के लिए 80000 रुपये का निवेश किया

ख्याति ने 6 महीने के लिए 30000 रुपये का निवेश किया

वर्ष के अंत में लाभ = 25500 रुपये

प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

लाभ = पूंजी × समयावधि

गणना:

सपना और ख्याति द्वारा निवेश की गई पूंजी का अनुपात

⇒ (12 × 80000) ∶ (6 × 30000)

= 960000 ∶ 180000 = 16 ∶ 3

निवेश की गई पूंजी का अनुपात = लाभ में हिस्सेदारी का अनुपात

ख्याति का लाभ = (3/19) × 25500 = रु. 4026.31 ≈ 4026 रुपये

अतः, लाभ में ख्याति का हिस्सा 4026 रुपये है।

textEn: Ram can do 50% of a work in 24 days, Shyam can do 1/4<sup>th</sup> of the work in 36 day. In how many days can they do 1/6<sup>th</sup> of the work working together.
textHi: राम एक कार्य का 50%, 24 दिनों में कर सकता है, श्याम 1/4 कार्य को 36 दिनों में कर सकता है। वे एक साथ कार्य करते हुए कार्य का 1/6 भाग कितने दिनों में कर सकते हैं?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Ram's 100% work time = 24 × 2 = 48 days; Shyam's 100% work time = 36 × 4 = 144 days.

Total work = LCM of 48 and 144 = 144 units.

Combined efficiency = (144 ÷ 48) + (144 ÷ 144) = 3 + 1 = 4 units/day.

Required work to complete = 1/6 × 144 = 24 units.

Time required = 24 units ÷ 4 units/day = 6 days.

∴ The correct answer is 6 days.

Alternate Method

Given:

Ram completes 50% (or 1/2) of the work in 24 days.

Shyam completes 1/4th of the work in 36 days.

Formula Used:

Total Work = Efficiency × Time

Calculations:

⇒ Total time for Ram to complete 100% work = 24 × 2 = 48 days

⇒ Total time for Shyam to complete 100% work = 36 × 4 = 144 days

Person Days Total Work Efficiency
Ram 48 144 3
Shyam 144 1

⇒ Combined efficiency of Ram and Shyam = 3 + 1 = 4 units/day

⇒ Required work to be done = 1/6th of total work

⇒ Required work = 144 ÷ 6 = 24 units

⇒ Time required = Required work ÷ Combined efficiency

⇒ Time required = 24 ÷ 4 = 6 days

∴ The correct answer is 6 days.

Additional Information

Pipes & Cisterns

Works exactly like Time & Work. Inlet pipes have positive efficiency, while outlet pipes (leaks) have negative efficiency. Capacity replaces total work.

Work & Wages

Wages are distributed in the direct ratio of the total work done by each person. If everyone works for the same number of days, wages are divided based on their daily efficiencies.

Alternate Days Work

To solve alternate days problems, calculate the total work done in one complete cycle (e.g., 2 days for 2 people) and multiply it to reach just below the total work before finishing the rest sequentially.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

राम 50% कार्य को 24 दिनों में कर सकता है।

श्याम 1/4 कार्य को 36 दिनों में कर सकता है।

गणना:

50% का भिन्नात्मक मान = 1/2

राम 1/2 कार्य को 24 दिनों में कर सकता है, तो पूरा कार्य 48 दिनों में पूरा होता है।

श्याम कार्य का 1/4 भाग 36 दिनों में और पूरा कार्य 144 दिनों में कर सकता है।

इस प्रकार, राम और श्याम एकसाथ मिलकर कार्य का 1/6 भाग \(144 × \frac{1}{6}\over 4 \) = 6 दिन में कर सकते हैं।

Alternate Methodराम के लिए

श्याम के लिए,

25% कार्य 36 दिनों में पूरा किया जाता है।

राम और श्याम एक साथ

राम और श्याम एकसाथ मिलकर कार्य का 1/6 भाग 36 × 1/6 = 6 दिनों में कर सकते हैं।

textEn: A pump can fill a tank with water in 3 hours. Because of a leak it took \(3\frac{1}{5}hours\) to fill the tank. The leak can drain all the water in the tank in
textHi: एक पंप किसी पानी की टंकी को 3 घंटे में भरता है। रिसाव के कारण टंकी भरने में \(3\frac{1}{5}\) घंटे लगे। इस रिसाव के कारण कितनी देर में टंकी पूरी खाली हो सकती है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Total Capacity = LCM of numerators of 3 and 16/5 = 48 units.

Efficiency of Pump = 48 ÷ 3 = 16 units/hr.

Combined Efficiency of Pump + Leak = 48 ÷ (16/5) = 15 units/hr.

Efficiency of Leak = 16 − 15 = 1 unit/hr (draining).

Time taken by Leak alone = Total Capacity ÷ Leak Efficiency = 48 ÷ 1 = 48 hours.

∴ The correct answer is 48 hours.

Alternate Method

Given:

Time taken by the pump to fill the tank = 3 hours

Time taken to fill the tank with a leak = 31/5 hours = 16/5 hours

Method Used:

We use the LCM method to find the total capacity of the tank and individual efficiencies.

Pipe/Leak Hours Total Capacity Efficiency
Pump A 3 48 16
Pump A + Leak B 16/5 15

Calculations:

⇒ Efficiency of Leak B = Combined Efficiency − Efficiency of Pump A

⇒ Efficiency of Leak B = 15 − 16 = −1 unit/hour

⇒ The negative sign indicates that Leak B is draining the water at 1 unit/hour.

⇒ Time taken by Leak B to drain the full tank = Total Capacity ÷ Draining Efficiency

⇒ Time taken = 48 ÷ 1 = 48 hours

∴ The correct answer is 48 hours.

Additional Information

Inlet and Outlet Pipes

An inlet pipe fills the tank (positive work), while an outlet pipe or leak drains the tank (negative work).

Combined Work Formula

If pipe A fills a tank in x hours and pipe B empties it in y hours (where y > x), the net part filled in 1 hour is (1/x − 1/y).

Total Time to Fill/Empty

Total time = Total Capacity ÷ Net Efficiency. If net efficiency is positive, the tank gets filled. If negative, it gets emptied.
explanationHi:
पंप इस टंकी को 3 घंटे में भरता है

∴ एक घंटे में भरा जाने वाला टंकी का हिस्सा = 1/3

माना, रिसाव के कारण टंकी 'x' घंटे में खाली हो जायेगी

∴ रिसाव के कारण 1 घंटे में टंकी का खाली होने वाला हिस्सा = 1/x

दिया गया है कि रिसाव के कारण पंप को टंकी भरने में \(3\frac{1}{5}hours = \frac{{16}}{5}hours\) लगे

1/3 - 1/x = 5/16

⇒ 1/x = 1/3 - 5/16

⇒ 1/x = (16 - 15)/48

⇒ 1/x = 1/48

⇒ x = 48 घंटे

3 और 16/5 दोनों को 5 से गुणा करने पर = 15 और 16 घंटे

15 और 16 घंटे का लघुत्तम समापवर्त्य = 240 इकाई

कुल कार्य = 240 इकाई

पंप की दक्षता = 240/15 = 16 इकाई/घंटा

पंप की दक्षता + रिसाव = 240/16 = 15 घंटे

रिसाव की दक्षता = 16 - 15 = 1

रिसाव द्वारा खाली होने में लिया गया समय = 240/1 = 240 घंटे

अब, 240 को 5 से विभाजित करने पर = 48 घंटे (हमने शुरू होने वाले घंटों को 5 से गुणा कर दिया है)

∴ रिसाव को टंकी खाली करने में 48 घंटे लगते हैं।

Shortcut Trick 3 और 16/5 का LCM = अंश का LCM/ हर का HCF = 48/1 = 48 इकाई

माना B टंकी में रिसाव है।

रिसाव की दक्षता = 16 - 15 = 1 इकाई/घंटा

रिसाव द्वारा टंकी को खाली करने में लगा समय = 48 इकाई/1 = 48 घंटे

textEn: Find the value of \(\sqrt{\frac{1-\tan A}{1+\tan A}}\).
textHi: \(\sqrt{\frac{1-\tan A}{1+\tan A}}\) का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Put A = 30° in the given expression.

⇒ √[(1 − tan 30°) / (1 + tan 30°)] = √[(1 − 1/√3) / (1 + 1/√3)] = √[(√3 − 1) / (√3 + 1)]

⇒ Multiply numerator and denominator by (√3 − 1) to get √(2 − √3).

Now, substitute A = 30° in the correct expression:

⇒ √[(1 − sin 60°) / cos 60°] = √[(1 − √3/2) / (1/2)] = √(2 − √3)

∴ The correct answer is √[(1 − sin 2A) / cos 2A].

Alternate Method

Given:

Expression = √[(1 − tan A) / (1 + tan A)]

Formula Used:

tan A = sin A / cos A

sin2 A + cos2 A = 1

sin 2A = 2 × sin A × cos A

cos 2A = cos2 A − sin2 A

Calculations:

⇒ (1 − tan A) / (1 + tan A) = [1 − (sin A / cos A)] / [1 + (sin A / cos A)]

⇒ = (cos A − sin A) / (cos A + sin A)

⇒ Multiply numerator and denominator by (cos A − sin A):

⇒ = (cos A − sin A)2 / [(cos A + sin A)(cos A − sin A)]

⇒ = (cos2 A + sin2 A − 2 × sin A × cos A) / (cos2 A − sin2 A)

⇒ Substitute the trigonometric identities:

⇒ = (1 − sin 2A) / cos 2A

⇒ Taking the square root of the simplified expression:

⇒ √[(1 − tan A) / (1 + tan A)] = √[(1 − sin 2A) / cos 2A]

∴ The correct answer is √[(1 − sin 2A) / cos 2A].

Additional Information

Tangent Difference Identity

tan(45° − A) = (1 − tan A) / (1 + tan A)

Double Angle Formulas

sin 2A = 2 × sin A × cos A and cos 2A = cos2 A − sin2 A

Pythagorean Identity

sin2 A + cos2 A = 1
explanationHi:
दिया गया है कि:

\(\sqrt{\frac{1-\tan A}{1+\tan A}}\)

प्रयुक्त संकल्पना:

tan A = sinA/cosA

sin2 A + cos2 A = 1

cos2 A - sin2 A = cos 2A

2cosAsinA = sin2A

गणना:

\(\sqrt{\frac{1-\tan A}{1+\tan A}}\)

⇒ \(\sqrt{\frac{1-\frac{sinA}{cosA}}{1+\frac{sinA}{cosA}}}\)

⇒ \(\sqrt{\frac{\frac{cosA-sinA}{cosA}}{\frac{cosA+sinA}{cosA}}}\)

⇒ \(\sqrt{\frac{cosA-sinA}{cosA+sinA}}\)

⇒ \(\sqrt{\frac{(cosA-sinA)(cosA-sinA)}{(cosA+sinA)(cosA-sinA)}}\)

⇒ \(\sqrt{\frac{(cosA-sinA)^2}{(cosA^2-sinA^2)}}\)

⇒ \(\sqrt{\frac{(cosA^2+sinA^2-2cosAsinA)}{cos2A}}\)

⇒ \(\sqrt{\frac{(1-sin2A)}{cos2A}}\)

∴ विकल्प 2 सही उत्तर है।

textEn: Which is the largest number that divides 827, 1149 and 1310 to leave the same remainder in each case?
textHi: वह सबसे बड़ी संख्या कौन सी है जिससे 827, 1149 और 1310 को विभाजित करने पर प्रत्येक स्थिति में समान शेषफल प्राप्त होता है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

The largest number dividing a, b, and c leaving the same remainder = HCF of |a − b|, |b − c|, |c − a|.

⇒ Difference 1 = |1149 − 827| = 322

⇒ Difference 2 = |1310 − 1149| = 161

⇒ Since 322 is a multiple of 161, their HCF is simply 161.

∴ The correct answer is 161.

Alternate Method

Given:

The numbers are 827, 1149, and 1310.

Formula Used:

When the largest number divides x, y, and z to leave the same remainder in each case, the number is given by the HCF of their absolute differences: HCF(|x − y|, |y − z|, |z − x|).

Calculation:

⇒ First difference = |1149 − 827| = 322

⇒ Second difference = |1310 − 1149| = 161

⇒ Third difference = |1310 − 827| = 483

⇒ Now, we need to find the HCF of 322, 161, and 483.

⇒ Factorising the numbers:

⇒ 161 = 161 × 1

⇒ 322 = 161 × 2

⇒ 483 = 161 × 3

⇒ The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is clearly 161.

∴ The correct answer is 161.

Additional Information

Case 1: Different Remainders

To find the largest number that divides x, y, and z leaving remainders a, b, and c respectively, calculate the HCF of (x − a), (y − b), and (z − c).

Case 2: Same Known Remainder

To find the largest number that divides x, y, and z leaving the same given remainder r in each case, calculate the HCF of (x − r), (y − r), and (z − r).

LCM Application

To find the smallest number which when divided by x, y, and z leaves the same remainder r, the formula is LCM(x, y, z) + r.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

संख्या = 827, 1149 और 1310

प्रयुक्त अवधारणा:

महत्तम समापवर्तक - वह सबसे बड़ी संख्या या मात्रा जो संख्याओं या मात्राओं के समूह के प्रत्येक सदस्य का गुणनखंड होता है

गणना:

दी गई संख्या का सापेक्ष अंतर लेने पर

827, 1149 और 1310 का महत्तम समापवर्तक

⇒ (1149 – 827) = 322

⇒ (1310 – 1149) = 161

⇒ (1310 – 827) = 483

अब,

322, 161 और 483 का महत्तम समापवर्तक

⇒ 322 = 2 × 161

⇒ 161 = 1 × 161

⇒ 483 = 3 × 161

महत्तम समापवर्तक 161 है

∴ वह सबसे बड़ी संख्या जिससे 827, 1149 और 1310 को विभाजित करने पर प्रत्येक स्थिति में समान शेषफल प्राप्त होता है, वह 161 हैI

textEn: The average weight of 14 persons is 76.5 kg. If 3 more persons of weight 82 kg, 75 kg and x kg join them, then the average weight of all the persons increases by 0.5 kg. What is the value of x?
textHi: 14 व्यक्तियों का औसत भार 76.5 किग्रा है। यदि 82 किग्रा, 75 किग्रा और x किग्रा भार के 3 और व्यक्ति उनके साथ जुड़ जाते हैं, तो सभी व्यक्तियों का औसत भार 0.5 किग्रा बढ़ जाता है। x का मान क्या है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Net increase in total weight = Total persons × Increase in average = 17 × 0.5 = 8.5 kg

Sum of weights of 3 new persons = (3 × 76.5) + 8.5 = 229.5 + 8.5 = 238 kg

Given weights are 82 kg, 75 kg, and x kg ⇒ 82 + 75 + x = 238

⇒ 157 + x = 238 ⇒ x = 81

∴ The correct answer is 81 kg.

Alternate Method

Given:

Average weight of 14 persons = 76.5 kg

Number of new persons = 3

Increase in average weight = 0.5 kg

Formula Used:

Sum of observations = Average × Number of observations

Calculations:

⇒ Total weight of 14 persons = 14 × 76.5 = 1071 kg

⇒ Total number of persons after 3 join = 14 + 3 = 17 persons

⇒ New average weight = 76.5 + 0.5 = 77 kg

⇒ Total weight of 17 persons = 17 × 77 = 1309 kg

⇒ Sum of weights of the 3 new persons = 1309 − 1071 = 238 kg

⇒ 82 + 75 + x = 238

⇒ 157 + x = 238

⇒ x = 238 − 157 = 81

∴ The correct answer is 81 kg.

Additional Information

Weighted Average

When two groups with averages A1 and A2 and sizes n1 and n2 are combined, the combined average is (n1 × A1 + n2 × A2) ÷ (n1 + n2).

Net Deviation Method

The sum of deviations of all individual values from their average is always zero. Adding new items increases the overall average only if their combined value exceeds the original average.

Average of a Group

Average = Sum of all observations ÷ Total number of observations.
explanationHi:
सूत्र:

औसत भार = सभी व्यक्तियों का कुल भार ÷ व्यक्तियों की कुल संख्या

गणना:

व्यक्तियों का कुल भार = 14 × 76.5 = 1071 किग्रा

बढ़ा हुआ कुल भार = 82 + 75 + x = (157 + x) किग्रा

व्यक्तियों का नया कुल भार = 17 × 77 = 1309 किग्रा

प्रश्न के अनुसार-

⇒ 1071 + 157 + x = 1309

⇒ x = 1309 - (1071 + 157)

⇒ x = 81 किग्रा

∴ x का मान 81 किग्रा है।

textEn: Two objects were sold for ₹9,900 each. One object was sold at a loss of 10% and the other object was sold at a gain of 10%. The net gain or loss in the whole transaction is:
textHi: दो वस्तुओं, प्रत्येक को ₹9,900 में बेचा जाता है। एक वस्तु को 10% की हानि पर बेचा जाता है और दूसरी वस्तु को 10% के लाभ पर बेचा जाता है। पूरे लेनदेन में कुल लाभ या हानि ज्ञात कीजिए।
optionsHi: ₹1,000 का लाभ
₹ 200 की हानि
<font style="vertical-align: inherit;"><font style="vertical-align: inherit;">₹600 की हानि </font></font>
₹100 का लाभ
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

When two objects are sold at the same SP with x% profit and x% loss, there is always a net loss of x2 ÷ 100%.

Net Loss % = (10 × 10) ÷ 100 = 1%.

Total SP = 9900 × 2 = ₹19,800, which is equivalent to 99% of Total CP (100% − 1%).

Loss amount = 1% of CP = (19800 ÷ 99) × 1 = ₹200.

∴ The correct answer is Loss ₹200.

Alternate Method

Given:

SP of first object = ₹9,900, Profit = 10%

SP of second object = ₹9,900, Loss = 10%

Formula Used:

CP = [100 ÷ (100 ± Profit/Loss %)] × SP

Loss = Total CP − Total SP

Calculations:

⇒ CP of first object = [100 ÷ (100 + 10)] × 9900

⇒ CP1 = (100 ÷ 110) × 9900 = ₹9,000

⇒ CP of second object = [100 ÷ (100 − 10)] × 9900

⇒ CP2 = (100 ÷ 90) × 9900 = ₹11,000

⇒ Total CP = 9000 + 11000 = ₹20,000

⇒ Total SP = 9900 + 9900 = ₹19,800

⇒ Net Loss = Total CP − Total SP

⇒ Net Loss = 20000 − 19800 = ₹200

∴ The correct answer is Loss ₹200.

Additional Information

Same Selling Price Condition

When two items are sold at the same price, one at a profit of x% and the other at a loss of x%, there is always an overall loss of (x2 ÷ 100)%.

Cost Price Formula

CP can be directly calculated from SP using: CP = SP × [100 ÷ (100 ± R)], where R is the profit or loss percentage.

Successive Discounts

If two successive discounts of a% and b% are given, the equivalent single discount is (a + b − ab ÷ 100)%.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है

प्रत्येक वस्तु का विक्रय मूल्य = ₹9,900

पहली वस्तु पर हानि = 10%

दूसरी वस्तु पर लाभ = 10%

अवधारणा:

यदि कोई वस्तु a% की हानि पर बेची जाती है, तो क्रय मूल्य, विक्रय मूल्य/(1 - a/100) होता है। यदि a% के लाभ पर बेचा जाता है, तो क्रय मूल्य, विक्रय मूल्य/(1 + a/100) होता है।

हल:

⇒ पहली वस्तु का क्रय मूल्य = ₹9,900/(1 - 10/100) = ₹11,000

⇒ दूसरी वस्तु का क्रय मूल्य = ₹9,900/(1 + 10/100) = ₹9,000

⇒ कुल क्रय मूल्य = ₹11,000 + ₹9,000 = ₹20,000

⇒ कुल विक्रय मूल्य = 2 × ₹9,900 = ₹19,800

⇒ हानि = कुल क्रय मूल्य - कुल विक्रय मूल्य = ₹20,000 - ₹19,800 = ₹200

इसलिए, पूरे लेनदेन में ₹200 की कुल हानि हुई।

textEn: A triangle and a parallelogram are on the same base and between the same parallels. The ratio of the area of triangle and parallelogram is _______.
textHi: एक त्रिभुज तथा समानांतर चतुर्भुज समान आधार पर तथा समान समानांतर रेखाओं के बीच स्थित हैं। त्रिभुज तथा समानांतर चतुर्भुज के क्षेत्रफल का अनुपात ________ है।
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

By geometric properties, a triangle and a parallelogram on the same base and between the same parallel lines share equal base length and perpendicular height.

Area of triangle = ½ × base × height

Area of parallelogram = base × height

Required Ratio = (½ × base × height) ÷ (base × height) = 1 ÷ 2

∴ The correct answer is 1 : 2.

Alternate Method

Given:

A triangle and a parallelogram lie on the identical base (b) and strictly between the exact same parallel boundaries.

Formula Used:

Area of a Parallelogram = base × height

Area of a Triangle = ½ × base × height

Calculation:

⇒ Let the shared base of both geometric shapes be 'b'.

⇒ Because they are positioned between the identical pair of parallel lines, the perpendicular height 'h' remains the same for both.

⇒ Area of Parallelogram = b × h

⇒ Area of Triangle = ½ × b × h

⇒ Ratio of Area (Triangle to Parallelogram) = (½ × b × h) ÷ (b × h)

⇒ Ratio = ½ = 1 : 2

∴ The correct answer is 1 : 2.

Additional Information

Parallelograms on the Same Base

Parallelograms lying on the same base and structurally between the same parallel lines strictly maintain an equal total area relative to each other.

Triangles on the Same Base

Triangles constructed on the exact same base and remaining between the identical parallel lines invariably possess equal area measurements.

Median Area Division

A median traced from any given vertex of a triangle correctly halves the opposing side, thereby bisecting the primary triangle into two individual triangles carrying equal areas.
explanationHi:
गणना:

समान आधार और समान समानांतर रेखाओं के बीच, त्रिभुज और समानांतर चतुर्भुज

यदि त्रिभुज और समानांतर चतुर्भुज एक ही आधार पर और एक ही समानांतर रेखाओं के बीच हों, तो त्रिभुज का क्षेत्रफल समानांतर चतुर्भुज के क्षेत्रफल के आधे के बराबर होता है।


समानांतर चतुर्भुज ABCD और त्रिभुज ABD एक ही आधार AB पर और समान समांतर AF और DC के बीच हैं।

यहाँ, AB आधार है और AE त्रिभुज ABD की ऊँचाई है।

∴ ABD का क्षेत्रफल = 1/2 समानांतर चतुर्भुज ABCD का क्षेत्रफल

∴ ABD का क्षेत्रफल / समानांतर चतुर्भुज ABCD का क्षेत्रफल = 1 : 2

textEn: In the given figure, if AB || CD, then find x + y.
textHi: दी गई आकृति में, यदि AB || CD, तो x + y ज्ञात कीजिए।
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

For any zigzag transversal between two parallel lines, the sum of left-pointing angles equals the sum of right-pointing angles.

In the given figure, the transversals form right-pointing angles x and y, and a left-pointing angle of 60°.

Equating the angles pointing in opposite directions directly gives:

x + y = 60°

∴ The correct answer is 60°.

Alternate Method

Given:

Lines AB and CD are parallel (AB || CD).

The interior angle formed at the transversal vertex P is 60°.

The angles formed with the parallel lines are x and y.

Calculations:

⇒ Let us draw an imaginary line XY parallel to AB and CD passing through the point P, such that PX points to the left.

⇒ This line divides the 60° angle at P into two parts: ∠EPX (upper half) and ∠FPX (lower half).

⇒ Since AB || XY, the alternate interior angles formed by the transversal EP are equal:

⇒ ∠EPX = ∠BEP = x

⇒ Since CD || XY, the alternate interior angles formed by the transversal FP are equal:

⇒ ∠FPX = ∠DFP = y

⇒ The total angle on the left side of point P is given as 60°.

⇒ ∴ ∠EPX + ∠FPX = 60°

⇒ Substituting the corresponding values:

⇒ x + y = 60°

∴ The correct answer is 60°.

Additional Information

Parallel Lines & Transversals

When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, alternate interior angles are equal, corresponding angles are equal, and the sum of consecutive interior angles on the same side is exactly 180°.

Zigzag Transversal Rule

If two parallel lines are intersected by one or more zigzag transversals, the sum of all the angles pointing to the left is always equal to the sum of all the angles pointing to the right.

Linear Pair Axiom

If a ray stands on a straight line, the sum of the two adjacent angles formed is 180°. These angles are supplementary and establish a linear pair.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

AB || CD

प्रयुक्त अवधारणा:

(1) त्रिभुज के कोणों का योग = 180°

(2) जब दो रेखाएँ समानांतर होती हैं, तब संगत कोणों और एकांतर कोणों की अवधारणा लागू होती है।

गणना:

⇒ 30 + y = 40°

⇒ y = 10°

चूँकि, AB और EF रेखाएँ प्रतिच्छेद करती हैं इसलिए, ∠EOB = ∠AOF = 40° (शार्षाभिमुख कोण)

इसलिए त्रिभुज के कोणों का योग 180° होता है

⇒ 100° + 30° + x= 180°

⇒ x = 50°

अब,

⇒ x + y = 50° + 10°

⇒ x + y = 60°

∴ सही उत्तर 60° है।

textEn: The simplified value of (0.2)<sup>3</sup> × 400 ÷ 2000 of (0.2)<sup>2</sup> is:
textHi: (0.2)<sup>3</sup> x 400 ÷ 2000 of (0.2)<sup>2</sup> का सरलीकृत मान है:
explanationEn:
Given:

(0.2)3 × 400 ÷ 2000 of (0.2)2

Formula used:

BODMAS Rule (Bracket, Of, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction)

Calculations:

(0.2)3 × 400 ÷ 2000 of (0.2)2

⇒ 0.008 × 400 ÷ (2000 × 0.04)

⇒ 0.008 × 400 ÷ 80

⇒ 0.008 × 5

⇒ 0.04

⇒ \(\frac{4}{100}\)

⇒ \(\frac{1}{25}\)

∴ The correct answer is option (1).
explanationHi:

(0.2)3 × 400 ÷ 2000 का (0.2)2

प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

BODMAS नियम (कोष्ठक (Bracket), का (Of), भाग (Division), गुणा (Multiplication), जोड़ (Addition), घटाव (Subtraction))

गणना:

⇒ \(\frac{4}{100}\)

textEn: Shyam alone can do a piece of work in 40 days working 2 hours a day. Ram alone can do the same work in 60 days working 2 hours a day. How many hours per day do they need to work together to complete the same work in 12 days?
textHi: श्याम अकेले प्रतिदिन 2 घंटे कार्य करके एक कार्य को 40 दिन में कर सकता है l राम अकेले उसी कार्य को प्रतिदिन 2 घंटे कार्य करके 60 दिन में कर सकता है l उसी कार्य को 12 दिन में पूरा करने के लिए उन्हें प्रतिदिन साथ मिलकर कितने घंटे कार्य करने की आवश्यकता है?
explanationEn:
Shortcut Trick

Total time needed by Shyam = 40 × 2 = 80 hours and Ram = 60 × 2 = 120 hours.

Time taken by both working together = (80 × 120) ÷ (80 + 120) = 9600 ÷ 200 = 48 hours.

Hours per day required to complete the work in 12 days = 48 ÷ 12 = 4 hours.

∴ The correct answer is 4 hours.

Alternate Method

Given:

Shyam completes the work in 40 days working 2 hours per day.

Ram completes the work in 60 days working 2 hours per day.

Calculations:

⇒ Total hours taken by Shyam alone = 40 × 2 = 80 hours

⇒ Total hours taken by Ram alone = 60 × 2 = 120 hours

Let Total Work be the LCM of their individual times (80 and 120 hours).

Person Hours Total Work Efficiency
Shyam 80 240 240 ÷ 80 = 3
Ram 120 240 ÷ 120 = 2

⇒ Total efficiency of Shyam and Ram working together = 3 + 2 = 5 units/hour

⇒ Total time required to complete the work together = Total Work ÷ Combined Efficiency

⇒ Total time = 240 ÷ 5 = 48 hours

⇒ Given the target is to complete the same work together in 12 days.

⇒ Hours per day required = Total time in hours ÷ Target days

⇒ Hours per day = 48 ÷ 12 = 4 hours

∴ The correct answer is 4 hours.

Additional Information

Pipes & Cisterns

Follows the exact same LCM concept as Time & Work. Inlet pipes are considered positive efficiency, while outlet (leak/emptying) pipes are taken as negative efficiency.

Work & Wages

Wages are strictly distributed in proportion to the total actual work done by each person. If all persons start and finish work simultaneously, wages are distributed simply in the ratio of their daily efficiencies.

Alternate Day Work

When individuals work on alternate days, calculate the combined work done in one complete cycle (e.g., 2 days for 2 people). Multiply this cycle to reach just below total work, then calculate remaining work for the next individual's turn.
explanationHi:
दिया गया है:

श्याम अकेले दिन में 2 घंटे कार्य करके 40 दिनों में एक कार्य कर सकता है। राम अकेले उसी कार्य को 2 घंटे प्रतिदिन कार्य करके 60 दिनों में कर सकता है।

प्रयुक्त सूत्र:

कुल कार्य = दक्षता x दिनों की संख्या (कार्य पूरा करने में लगने वाला समय)

गणना:

श्याम प्रतिदिन 2 घंटे कार्य करके कार्य को 40 दिनों में पूरा करता है।

इसलिए, श्याम द्वारा कार्य को पूरा करने में लिए गए कुल घंटे = 40 × 2 = 80 घंटे

राम प्रतिदिन 2 घंटे कार्य करके कार्य को 60 दिनों में पूरा करता है।

तो, राम द्वारा कार्य को पूरा करने में लिए गए कुल घंटे = 60 × 2 = 120 घंटे

माना कुल कार्य = लघुत्तम समापवर्त्य (80, 120) = 240 इकाई

श्याम की दक्षता = 240/80 = 3 इकाई

राम की दक्षता = 240/120 = 2 इकाई

वे (3 + 2) = 5 इकाइयों की दक्षता के साथ 12 दिनों तक एक साथ कार्य करते हैं

इसलिए, उन्हें प्रतिदिन 240/(12 × 5) = 20/5 = 4 घंटे कार्य करना होगा

∴ 12 दिनों में समान कार्य को पूरा करने के लिए उन्हें साथ मिलकर प्रतिदिन 4 घंटे कार्य करने की आवश्यकता है।

    • Example: I might hit the sack now.
  • It doesn't imply any other activity such as reading, writing, or sitting.
  • It is often used in casual or informal conversations and indicates that the speaker is tired and wants to sleep.
  • Hence, the correct answer is 'Option 3'.

textHi:
Direction: The first and sixth sentences are marked as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences in each have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. This is followed by four options each suggesting a sequence of the sentences. Identify the most appropriate option.

explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'SQPR'.​Key Points

  • The passage talks about India being a colony of Britain, which ruled out any move to adjust the exchange rate. This sets the context for the following events that led to the banks being left with unrecoverable assets.

  • Sentence Q states that with devaluation made politically impossible, fall in export demand led to sustained and deep deflation. This led to a decrease in the demand for exports, causing a fall in export earnings and worsening the balance of payments situation.

  • Finally, the passage concludes by saying that the banks were left with unrecoverable assets, which implies that the economic situation had become quite dire. Therefore, the correct sequence is SQPR.

textEn:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

One should help one's grandparents.
textHi:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

One should help one's grandparents.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Grandparents should be helped'.

Key Points

  • The above sentence is in passive voice since it emphasizes the action or object of the sentence.
  • The above sentence is a special kind of sentence that is asked in exams and it needs to be learned as it is.
  • For the above sentence, this rule is to be followed:
    • Active voice: One should help one's grandparents.
    • Passive voice: Grandparents should be helped. (better alternative)
      One's grandparents should be helped.
  • The tense remains the same as the passive voice.
  • Here, the simple present tense is used.

Additional Information

  • ​'One's' is always followed by a noun.
  • If 'one' is used at the beginning of a sentence, then only that should be repeated again. For example:
    • One should help his grandparents.
    • One should help one's grandparents.
  • Only sentences with the transitive verb can be converted into passive voice and not the ones with an intransitive verb.
  • A transitive verb needs an object to complete its meaning. For example: He answered the questions confidently. (subject + verb + object)
  • The intransitive verb does not have an object. For example: He answered confidently. (subject + verb)

Correct sentence: "Grandparents should be helped".

textEn:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice:

She said to her brother, "Remember to bring your umbrella with you when you go to school because it looks like it might rain in the afternoon."
textHi:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect voice:

She said to her brother, "Remember to bring your umbrella with you when you go to school because it looks like it might rain in the afternoon."
optionsEn: She reminded her brother to remember to bring his umbrella with him when he went to school because it looked like it might rain in the afternoon.
She reminded her brother to bring his umbrella with him when he went to school because it looked like it might rain in the afternoon.
She told her brother to bring his umbrella with him when he goes to school because it looks like it might rain in the afternoon.
She reminded her brother to bring your umbrella with you when you go to school because it looks like it might rain in the afternoon.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is Option 2.
Key Points
  • Indirect Speech: She reminded her brother to bring his umbrella with him when he went to school because it looked like it might rain in the afternoon.
  • Key Conversions:
    • Reporting verb: "said to" changes to "reminded" (since it's a reminder/instruction).
    • Pronoun changes: "your" becomes "his", "you" becomes "he", "with you" becomes "with him".
    • Tense changes: "go" becomes "went", "looks" becomes "looked".
  • Punctuation and Format:
    • Quotation marks are removed in indirect speech.
    • The imperative ("Remember to bring") is converted to "reminded...to bring".
Therefore, the correct answer is Option 2.
Correct Sentence: She reminded her brother to bring his umbrella with him when he went to school because it looked like it might rain in the afternoon.
Additional Information
  • Option 1: "to remember to bring" is redundant; "reminded...to bring" is sufficient.
  • Option 3: Tense is not changed ("goes" and "looks" should be "went" and "looked").
  • Option 4: Pronouns not changed ("your", "you" remain instead of "his", "he").

textEn:
Fill up the blank with the correct option.

Nothing but huts __________ there.
textHi:
Fill up the blank with the correct option.

Nothing but huts __________ there.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "Is".

Key Points

  • The phrase 'Nothing but huts' is effectively treated as a singular subject because 'Nothing but' is one of the phrases mentioned in the rule that dictates the verb should agree with the first subject.
  • In this case, 'Nothing' is considered the first subject, which is singular.
  • The verb 'is' agrees with the singular noun 'Nothing,' which is technically the focus of the subject in the sentence, despite referring to multiple items (huts).
  • This rule overrides the typical plural agreement you might expect with the plural noun 'huts' because the construction with 'Nothing but' dictates singular agreement.
  • Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 3".

Additional Information

  • Were (Option 1): Would typically be the correct choice for a plural subject ('huts') if not for the specific rule applied here.
  • Are (Option 2): Is the plural present form of 'be' and would align with 'huts' in a different construction without the overriding rule.
  • Have (Option 4): Indicates possession or experience but is irrelevant due to the subject-verb agreement rule highlighted.
